<!-- Generated by spec-lite | agent: plan | date: 2026-05-05 -->

# Plan: Run Reporting (HTML Export)

## 1. Overview

Add a **single-click "Export Report" button** to each of the three run-results surfaces in wave-client — Run Collection ([RunCollectionModal.tsx](packages/core/src/components/common/RunCollectionModal.tsx)), Flow Runner ([FlowResultsPanel.tsx](packages/core/src/components/flow/FlowResultsPanel.tsx)), and Test Lab ([TestResultsPanel.tsx](packages/core/src/components/testlab/TestResultsPanel.tsx)) — that downloads the latest run as a **self-contained, single-file, interactive HTML report** (CSS + JS + data inlined).

Scope is intentionally narrow for this initial phase:

- **In scope**: HTML export only; latest run only (no historical results); basic interactivity (expand/collapse + tabs); pure client-side generation in `packages/core`.
- **Out of scope** (deferred): JSON export; persistence of run results across sessions; in-app rendering of the report; flow graph visualization; search/sort/filter inside the report; PDF/print stylesheet; CI/server-side report generation.

The wave-client UI itself does **not** render the HTML — clicking Export immediately downloads the file. Generation happens at click-time from the existing in-memory run state; no new persistence layer is added.

## 2. High-Level Features

| FEAT-ID | Feature | Spec File | Status |
|---------|---------|-----------|--------|
| FEAT-001 | Generic `exportFile` adapter method on `IStorageAdapter` | [features/feature_run_reporting_adapter.md](features/feature_run_reporting_adapter.md) | [x] Complete |
| FEAT-002 | Reporting foundation — types, escapers, theme/JS, shared request-detail block | [features/feature_run_reporting_foundation.md](features/feature_run_reporting_foundation.md) | [x] Complete |
| FEAT-003 | Collection run HTML report (first end-to-end slice) | [features/feature_run_reporting_collection.md](features/feature_run_reporting_collection.md) | [x] Complete |
| FEAT-004 | Flow run HTML report | [features/feature_run_reporting_flow.md](features/feature_run_reporting_flow.md) | [x] Complete |
| FEAT-005 | Test suite run HTML report | [features/feature_run_reporting_testsuite.md](features/feature_run_reporting_testsuite.md) | [x] Complete |

**Decomposition strategy**: Vertical slicing with a small foundational base. FEAT-001 and FEAT-002 are foundational plumbing (adapter contract + pure utilities). FEAT-003 is the first complete end-to-end slice (collection run report demonstrable). FEAT-004 and FEAT-005 reuse the foundation and the request-detail block to add the remaining two report types incrementally.

## 3. Tech Stack Additions

No additions — see memory.

The plan deliberately uses only what is already available:

- HTML/CSS/JS authored as plain TypeScript template literals (no templating library, no React SSR).
- No new dependencies in `packages/core`.

## 4. Data Model (High-Level)

Not applicable. This plan introduces no persisted entities. All inputs are read from existing in-memory state ([useCollectionRunner.tsx](packages/core/src/hooks/useCollectionRunner.tsx), the `flowRunStates` and `testSuiteRunStates` Zustand slices) and consumed transiently to produce an HTML string.

## 5. Interface Design

### 5.1 New Adapter Method (FEAT-001)

Extend `IStorageAdapter` in [packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts](packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts):

```ts
exportFile(
  fileName: string,
  content: string,
  mimeType: string
): Promise<Result<{ filePath: string; fileName: string }, string>>;
```

- **VS Code**: handler in `MessageHandler` opens a save dialog with the suggested filename, writes via `fs.writeFile`, returns the chosen path.
- **Web**: `webAdapter` creates a `Blob` with the supplied `mimeType`, attaches it to a hidden `a[download]` element, triggers click, revokes the object URL. Returns `{ fileName, filePath: '' }` (web has no path).

### 5.2 New Hook (introduced in FEAT-003, extended in FEAT-004 and FEAT-005)

`useReportExport()` in `packages/core/src/hooks/useReportExport.ts`:

- Local state: `status: 'idle' | 'generating' | 'success' | 'error'`, `error: string | undefined`.
- Methods (added per slice):
  - `exportCollectionRun(input)` — FEAT-003
  - `exportFlowRun(input)` — FEAT-004
  - `exportTestSuiteRun(input)` — FEAT-005
- Each method: build HTML string → call `adapter.storage.exportFile(...)` → update local status → surface error via `useNotificationAdapter()` on failure (matches memory's error-handling rule).

### 5.3 UI Integration

A small Export button (Lucide `DownloadIcon`) is added to each results surface:

- **Run Collection** ([RunCollectionModal.tsx](packages/core/src/components/common/RunCollectionModal.tsx)) — top toolbar, beside Run/Stop. Disabled while runner is `isRunning` or no results exist.
- **Flow Results** ([FlowResultsPanel.tsx](packages/core/src/components/flow/FlowResultsPanel.tsx)) — header row beside the existing Clear Results trash icon. Disabled when `result === null` or `status === 'running'`.
- **Test Suite Results** ([TestResultsPanel.tsx](packages/core/src/components/testlab/TestResultsPanel.tsx)) — same row as the existing Clear Results control. Disabled when `result === null` or `status === 'running'`.

Each button shows a brief inline status while generating (small spinner via Lucide `Loader2`) and surfaces success/failure through the existing notification adapter.

### 5.4 Filename Convention

`wave-{type}-{sanitized-name}-{YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS}.html`

- `type` ∈ `collection | flow | testsuite`.
- `sanitized-name` strips characters not in `[A-Za-z0-9-_]` and collapses runs of `-`.
- Timestamp is local time at click moment.

### 5.5 Report Layout (all three types share this skeleton)

1. **Header band** — Wave Client wordmark, report title, run type, started/completed timestamps, total elapsed.
2. **Run metadata strip** — environment name (or `None`), default auth name (or `None`), concurrency, delay-between-calls. (Auth: option a — chosen default auth name only, not per-request resolved auth.)
3. **Summary tiles** — Total / Passed / Failed / Skipped (where applicable) / Avg time. Same shape as the corresponding panel.
4. **Item list** — collapsed by default, click to expand. Each item exposes three tabs:
   - **Request** — method, URL, query params, headers, body (raw text).
   - **Response** — status, elapsed, headers, body. Binary/non-text bodies are **omitted** with a "(binary content not included)" placeholder; size is **not** truncated.
   - **Validation** — per-rule pass/fail breakdown (when validation ran).
5. **Type-specific nesting**:
   - **Collection** (FEAT-003): flat list of selected requests, in the order returned by `flattenRequests()`.
   - **Flow** (FEAT-004): flat ordered list of nodes (no graph diagram), nesting matches `FlowResultsPanel`.
   - **Test Suite** (FEAT-005): full nesting matching `TestResultsPanel` — request items expand to test cases (data-driven), flow items expand to test cases or to the node list, flow test cases expand to their flow node list.

### 5.6 Interactivity (v1)

- Click an item header to toggle expand/collapse.
- Click a tab inside an expanded item to switch between Request / Response / Validation.
- That is the entire interactivity surface. No search, no sort, no filter, no theme toggle.

## 6. Security Considerations

The HTML report is generated from user-supplied or remote data (URLs, headers, request/response bodies, validation messages). Treat **every** field that lands in the HTML as untrusted.

- A single, well-tested `escapeHtml` and `escapeAttr` pair lives in `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/escape.ts`. **No raw concatenation of dynamic strings into HTML anywhere.** Builders MUST route every dynamic value through these helpers.
- Response bodies render inside `<pre>` blocks via `escapeHtml(body)`. JSON bodies are pretty-printed *before* escaping; the escaped string is what gets written to HTML.
- The inline JS module (the click handlers) is a static string constant — it never interpolates dynamic data. Data-to-DOM coupling is achieved exclusively via `data-*` attributes that point to indexed `<template>`/`<pre>` content already rendered as escaped HTML.
- Headers and URL strings are escaped both as text content and (where applicable) as attribute values.
- Memory's standing security rules ([memory.md](.spec-lite/memory.md) → Security) continue to apply — never log secrets/tokens; do not include auth credential values in the report header (only the auth profile *name*).

No additions beyond memory for adapter security; the new `exportFile` method is bounded by the OS save dialog (VS Code) or browser download permission (Web).

## 7. Architecture & Design (Plan-Specific)

### 7.1 Module Layout

```
packages/core/src/utils/reporting/
├── index.ts                  # public exports
├── types.ts                  # ReportMetadata, ReportSummary, builder input types
├── escape.ts                 # escapeHtml, escapeAttr
├── format.ts                 # formatTimestamp, formatDuration, sanitizeFilename
├── content.ts                # isTextResponse (binary detection from headers/body)
├── theme.ts                  # CSS module — exported as a single string constant
├── interactivity.ts          # JS module — exported as a single string constant
├── blocks/
│   ├── header.ts             # renderHeader(metadata) → string
│   ├── summary.ts            # renderSummary(summary) → string
│   └── requestDetail.ts      # renderRequestDetail(RunRequestData) → string  (FEAT-002)
├── builders/
│   ├── collectionRun.ts      # buildCollectionRunReport(input) → string      (FEAT-003)
│   ├── flowRun.ts            # buildFlowRunReport(input) → string            (FEAT-004)
│   └── testSuiteRun.ts       # buildTestSuiteRunReport(input) → string      (FEAT-005)
└── shell.ts                  # renderShell({ title, body }) — wraps theme + interactivity around content
```

### 7.2 Plan-Specific Decisions

- **Pure-function generator**: every module under `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/` is a pure TypeScript module with no I/O, no DOM access, no async. Builders accept fully-resolved inputs and return a string. This makes them trivially testable and lets us lift the module to `packages/shared` later without rewrites if server-side generation is ever needed.
- **Single shared request-detail block** (`renderRequestDetail`) is the contract that lets all three report types share visual consistency with `RunRequestCard` without re-implementing the layout per type. Builders only differ in their *outer* structure (flat list vs nested test-case tree).
- **Inline-only assets**: the produced HTML is one file. Theme CSS and interactivity JS are inlined as `<style>` and `<script>` blocks. No external fetches, no `<link>`, no CDN. The file works fully offline.
- **No React SSR**: the report has no React lifecycle, no hydration, no virtual DOM — using `react-dom/server` would bloat the core bundle without benefit.
- **Adapter wraps file I/O**: `exportFile` is the single I/O boundary, conforming to the standing rule that core does no platform-specific I/O directly.
- **Hook owns export status**: per the user's request, status (`idle | generating | success | error`) and error message are local state inside `useReportExport`, surfaced through the existing notification adapter on failure rather than a global Zustand slice (no global concern, ephemeral).

### 7.3 Key Existing Files (read-only references)

- [packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts](packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts) — `IStorageAdapter` lives here; FEAT-001 extends it.
- [packages/core/src/hooks/useCollectionRunner.tsx](packages/core/src/hooks/useCollectionRunner.tsx) — `CollectionRunResult` shape.
- [packages/core/src/types/flow.ts](packages/core/src/types/flow.ts) — `FlowRunResult`, `FlowNodeResult`.
- [packages/core/src/types/testSuite.ts](packages/core/src/types/testSuite.ts) — `TestSuiteRunResult`, `TestItemResult`, `TestCaseResult`.
- [packages/core/src/components/common/RunRequestCard.tsx](packages/core/src/components/common/RunRequestCard.tsx) — visual reference for `renderRequestDetail` layout (Request / Response / Validation tabs).
- [packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts](packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts) and the corresponding `MessageHandler` — pattern to follow for FEAT-001's vscode side.
- [packages/web/src/adapters/webAdapter.ts](packages/web/src/adapters/webAdapter.ts) — pattern for FEAT-001's web side.

## 8. Coding Standards (Plan-Specific Overrides)

No overrides — see memory.

One nuance worth calling out (not an override): every dynamic string concatenation into HTML must go through `escapeHtml` / `escapeAttr`. Reviewers should treat raw template-literal interpolation of dynamic data as a defect.

## 9. Testing Strategy (Plan-Specific)

In addition to memory's standing testing conventions:

- **`escape.ts`** — exhaustive unit tests for `<`, `>`, `&`, `"`, `'`, `␀`, surrogate pairs, mixed payloads, and attribute-context vs text-context separation.
- **`content.ts`** — `isTextResponse` table tests over MIME types (`application/json`, `text/*`, `application/xml`, `application/octet-stream`, image/audio/video, missing/empty Content-Type, base64-encoded responses where `isResponseEncoded === true`).
- **Builders** — for each of `buildCollectionRunReport`, `buildFlowRunReport`, `buildTestSuiteRunReport`:
  - Snapshot-style assertions on key structural anchors (presence of summary tile values, item count, expected `data-*` attributes), not full HTML diffs (which break too easily).
  - Specific XSS regression tests: feed `<script>alert(1)</script>` into request name, URL, header value, response body, validation message — assert the produced HTML contains the literal escaped form and *not* an executable `<script>` tag.
  - Binary response handling: feed a non-text response and assert the placeholder string is present and the raw body is not.
- **`useReportExport` hook** — tested via `renderHook` from `@testing-library/react`:
  - Status transitions on success path.
  - Status transitions on adapter failure path; assert notification adapter received `'error'`.
  - Disabled-state derivation (no result / running).
- **Adapter integration**:
  - VS Code `MessageHandler.exportFile`: mocked save dialog + `fs.writeFile`, assert path returned and propagated through.
  - Web `webAdapter.exportFile`: mock `URL.createObjectURL` / `revokeObjectURL` / `document.createElement('a')`; assert click triggered with correct download filename and that URL is revoked.
- **Panel integration** — for each of the three panels, assert the Export button exists, is disabled in the right states, and calls the corresponding hook method with the correct payload.

## 10. Logging Strategy (Plan-Specific)

No additions — see memory.

The export path is fully local and synchronous from the user's perspective; failures are surfaced through the existing notification adapter. The standing rule that errors are logged with context applies to the adapter layer (e.g., `MessageHandler.exportFile` should log save-dialog cancellation vs filesystem error distinctly so the dev log is actionable).
